generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-enabled sensor collecting real-time occupancy data for adaptive control systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor measuring indoor temperature for HVAC and energy optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor measuring indoor humidity levels for comfort and system control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance LightLevelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Light intensity sensor for adaptive lighting control systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightLevelDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionMeter : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Smart meter measuring real-time energy consumption across building systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance LightLevelDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightLevelSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionMeter
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically enriched Building Information Model serving as the core semantic backbone for static and dynamic data integration"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimToRuntimeConverter, EclipseHonoMiddleware, EclipseDittoStateManager
]

instance DeviceMetadataModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model containing device configurations, control logic, and middleware specifications embedded within BIM"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoMiddleware, EclipseDittoStateManager
]

instance ControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Domain-specific control semantics (e.g., lighting control logic from TwinLight project) embedded in BIM"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AdaptiveControlService, AutomatedCommissioningService
]

instance ThreeDVisualizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D representation in Unreal Engine 5 for immersive monitoring and simulation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngine5Renderer
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous stream of sensor data (temperature, occupancy, energy consumption) ingested via Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoMiddleware, EclipseDittoStateManager
]

instance SensorConfigurationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Device metadata including sensor/actuator configurations and control logic specifications"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DeviceMetadataModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoMiddleware, EclipseDittoStateManager
]

instance ControlPolicyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Reinforcement learning policies and adaptive control strategies from BOREALIS project"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ControlLogicModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AdaptiveControlService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoMiddleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware for real-time IoT data ingestion and normalization from physical assets"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance EclipseDittoStateManager : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital twin state management with bidirectional synchronization capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, AdaptiveControlService
]

instance UnrealEngine5Renderer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D visualization engine providing immersive monitoring and simulation capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:enables InteractiveVisualizationService
]

instance BimToRuntimeConverter : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering component converting BIM semantics to runtime artifacts"
    DTDFVocab:enables AutomatedCommissioningService, RuntimeConfigurationService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automates extraction of domain semantics from BIM and generation of runtime artifacts"
    DTDFVocab:enables AutomatedCommissioningService, RuntimeConfigurationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of building operational states using real-time sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeOperationalInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance and optimization recommendations based on operational data"
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceScheduleInsight, OptimizationRecommendationInsight
]

instance AdaptiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Reinforcement learning-based optimization for energy-efficient lighting and HVAC systems"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationAction, ControlPolicyUpdateAction
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D monitoring enabling facility managers to interact with the digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationDrivenInsight
]

instance AutomatedCommissioningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Embedding control logic into BIM models to streamline deployment and configuration"
    DTDFVocab:provides DeploymentAutomationAction
]

instance SimulationAndAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Performance modeling and scenario testing for evaluating refurbishment impacts"
    DTDFVocab:provides ScenarioAnalysisInsight
]

instance RuntimeConfigurationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of control policies and sensor thresholds without full redeployment"
    DTDFVocab:provides ConfigurationUpdateAction
]

instance CustomAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Modular integration of custom analytics modules through standardized extension points"
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomAnalyticsInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeOperationalInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current operational states including energy consumption, occupancy, and environmental conditions"
]

instance MaintenanceScheduleInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance schedules based on equipment performance data"
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Energy optimization recommendations derived from real-time operational data"
]

instance VisualizationDrivenInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization enabling facility managers to explore operational data spatially"
]

instance ScenarioAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Performance modeling results for evaluating refurbishment scenarios and operational changes"
]

instance CustomAnalyticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Domain-specific analytics results from integrated custom modules"
]

instance EnergyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of lighting and HVAC systems based on reinforcement learning policies"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlPolicyUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Iterative refinement of control policies based on operational feedback"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DeploymentAutomationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated commissioning of building control systems using embedded BIM semantics"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ConfigurationUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of sensor thresholds and control parameters without system redeployment"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System actuator for adaptive lighting systems with reinforcement learning capabilities"
]

instance HvacActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC control system actuator for energy-efficient climate regulation"
]

instance ShadingMechanismActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Automated shading system actuator for solar gain optimization"
]

instance SmartSensorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-enabled adaptive sensor/actuator hybrid device for localized control optimization"
]

// C10: Additional Models/Data
instance DeviceTwinModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Specialized twin representing individual sensors and actuators with bidirectional communication"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoStateManager
]

instance ControlTwinModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Domain-specific twin handling control logic for lighting, HVAC, and other systems"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoStateManager
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The interactions from the virtual world to the physical world involve **control signals** generated by the DT and transmitted back to the Building Control Systems (BCS) or IoT actuators. For example, the DT may adjust lighting levels (via **BOREALIS’ reinforcement learning policies**) or trigger HVAC responses based on real-time data analysis. The framework supports **automated commissioning** (as in **TwinLight**) by embedding control logic directly into BIM models, enabling seamless deployment of virtual-to-physical commands. Eclipse **Ditto** manages the state of digital twins and ensures synchronized updates to physical systems via middleware integration."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Real-time synchronization between physical assets and the DT, with continuous updates for operational monitoring and control. The DT also supports long-term lifecycle management, including design, construction, and operational phases."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as a **service-oriented architecture** with the following components:
  - **BIM Core**: Central semantic model integrating static and dynamic data.
  - **Middleware Layer**: Eclipse **Hono/Ditto** for real-time data exchange and state management.
  - **Control Layer**: Embedded within BIM (e.g., **TwinLight’s** control semantics or **BOREALIS’** RL policies).
  - **Visualization Layer**: **Unreal Engine 5** for interactive 3D monitoring.
  - **Extension Layer**: Custom services/plugins integrated via standardized interfaces.
  The constellation supports **modular deployment**, allowing components to be scaled or replaced independently. The framework ensures **interoperability** across heterogeneous systems via standardized protocols (e.g., MQTT, AMQP)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT covers the following life cycle stages of the physical building:
  1. **Design Phase**: Leverages BIM models to represent the **as-designed** state, including geometric, spatial, and functional attributes.
  2. **Construction Phase**: Integrates **as-manufactured** data (e.g., deviations from design) via BIM updates.
  3. **Operational Phase**: Synchronizes with **as-operated** data (e.g., sensor readings, maintenance logs) to reflect real-time conditions.
  4. **Refurbishment/Adaptation**: Supports dynamic updates to the DT to accommodate modifications (e.g., retrofitting, reconfigurations). The framework ensures continuity across phases by maintaining semantic consistency via BIM."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity is evaluated using the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**, which measures how well the DT reflects its physical counterpart. Validation involves ensuring semantic coherence, real-time synchronization, and alignment with operational requirements. Uncertainty is addressed through automated data normalization and bidirectional communication protocols."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Technical connections use **Eclipse Hono** for IoT data ingestion and **Ditto** for digital twin management, with standardized protocols for bidirectional communication. The network architecture supports real-time data exchange between physical assets and the DT."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is deployed using a **model-driven framework** that automates the generation of runtime artifacts (e.g., middleware configurations, visualization models) from enriched BIM models. The framework supports **scalable deployment** across heterogeneous infrastructure environments, reducing manual effort and enhancing maintainability. While the exact hosting environment (e.g., cloud or edge) is not specified, the architecture is designed for **modularity and extensibility**, allowing deployment on scalable platforms. The focus is on **automated, reproducible setups** to ensure consistency across projects."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with external systems is supported via standardized extension points and middleware (Hono/Ditto). This includes compatibility with other DTs, IoT platforms, and AECO-specific tools (e.g., BIM software). Interoperability is ensured through semantic modeling and automated transformations."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to **AECO-specific standards** such as **Building Information Modeling (BIM)** as the semantic backbone. It leverages **open-source middleware** (Eclipse Hono and Ditto) for standardized data exchange and integration. While specific standards like **IFC (Industry Foundation Classes)** or **COBie (Construction Operations Building Information Exchange)** are not explicitly mentioned, the framework emphasizes **interoperability** and **modularity** to align with broader industry standards. The use of **BIM** ensures compatibility with existing AECO workflows and tools."
]
